raw_consumer_statement:
in XX/XX/year> I purchased XXXX  from a pop up store in XXXX, GA, XXXX XXXX. Amount of {$10000.00}. I promptly returned the product the following day and had a heated discussion with the merchant. I left all o the product just as I had received it on the floor at this feet and immediately left. I immediately filed a dispute with AMEX, which they denied. So I am expected to pay the {$10000.00} for product that I no longer have. I contacted XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX ) the merchant. He informed that he closed his pop up shop and he would ask another merchant ( XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX in XXXX FL ). 
He sent me the replacement products. Then called me a couple of weeks later saying that XXXX had not been paid and now he ( XXXX XXXX has not been paid. He asked for my credit card and I gave him my AMEX #. Then I was charged {$10000.00} to my AMEX card. XXXX is still saying that he did not receive his payment, but I was told by AMEX that both merchants were in fact paid. And Now I am charged over {$20000.00} for was to be one product. I contacted both merchants time and time again, opened disputes time and time again. 
I asked for XXXX to send me a letter stating that I was due a refund from him for {$10000.00}. He send me a letter that he had certified, which I will include. I thought this would clear the matter up, however, when AMEX contacted XXXX, HE SAID THAT I FORGED THE NOTARIZED LETTER. Let me ask you this ... Who do you think is the honest person here? I have been an AMEX XXXX  card carrier for over 20 years. I am no liar. Did someone even contact the Notary, and did he lie also? 
This has destroyed my credit and has caused me much XXXX. I am XXXX XXXX XXXX and I feel that XXXX thinks he can XXXX me and that I will give up.
